meta.title: TypeScript Performance – Optimization Tips & Best Practices (2026)
meta.description: Learn how to write high-performance TypeScript code. Discover compiler options, optimization techniques, and common performance pitfalls.

content.value: TypeScript performance affects both compile-time and runtime. Optimizing your TypeScript code leads to faster builds, smaller bundles, and better user experiences.
Split large codebases into smaller projects to speed up compilation.

value: **Use primitive types** over objects when possible
**Avoid type assertions** that cause runtime checks
**Minimize spread operators** in hot code paths
**Use Map/Set** instead of plain objects for dynamic lookups

content.value:
# Generate performance trace
tsc --generateTrace trace

# Analyze with Chrome DevTools or
# https://typescript.azurewebsites.net/trace

# Measure compile time
time tsc --noEmit

# Use TypeScript compiler performance flags
tsc --extendedDiagnostics

value: **Set strict mode** to catch errors early
**Avoid barrel files** (index.ts that re-exports many modules)
**Prefer interfaces** over type aliases for object types

content.value: TypeScript performance optimization balances compile-time speed, bundle size, and runtime efficiency. Use compiler flags, project references, and type design patterns to keep your TypeScript code fast and maintainable.

faq.question: How to speed up TypeScript compilation?
faq.answer: Use incremental builds, skipLibCheck, and project references.

faq.question: What is incremental compilation?
faq.answer: Recompiles only changed files instead of the whole project.

faq.question: Do const assertions improve performance?
faq.answer: They improve type inference but don't affect runtime.
